Summary: In advanced non-small cell lung cancer, PD-L1 expression is the preferred biomarker for predicting the efficacy of immune checkpoint inhibitors. High PD-L1 positivity is often accompanied by high levels of T lymphocyte infiltration. Detection of PD-L1 in tumor tissues is the most direct method to predict response to PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors. A systematic review of 19 studies found that, for advanced NSCLC, most evidence shows patients with high PD-L1 expression benefited more from single-agent anti-PD-1/PD-L1 drugs (nivolumab, pembrolizumab, duvalizumab, atilizumab, and avelumab). PD-L1 expression level has become an important basis for clinicians to formulate immunotherapy programs for NSCLC patients. In NSCLC patients with high tumor mutational burden, first-line nivolumab plus ipilimumab significantly prolonged progression-free survival regardless of PD-L1 expression status.
References: Tang, S., Qin, C., Hu, H., Liu, T., He, Y., Guo, H., Yan, H., Zhang, J., Tang, S., & Zhou, H. (2022). Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: Progress, Challenges, and Prospects. Cells, 11(3), 320. https://doi.org/10.3390/cells11030320
